title: Dasystephana cruciata
taxonomy: Dasystephana cruciata is classified within the family Asteraceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as sunflowers or daisies. This plant is further classified within the subfamily Stifftioideae, a smaller group within the Asteraceae family. The tribe to which Dasystephana cruciata belongs is not specified in the information I have, but it's within the subfamily Stifftioideae. Dasystephana cruciata is native to Brazil and other parts of South America. The classification is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships.

intro: Dasystephana cruciata, also known as the "Catholic Crossflower", is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering relief from fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, making it a valuable resource for treating various health conditions.
